mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 2.0% and remains there for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following trigger activation are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 200,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance improves.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly change and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window, or positive payroll surprises do not precede positive equity returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that compresses earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reprice growth risk.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST falls > 5% month-over-month simultaneously
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 1.5% year-over-year signals supply-side capacity expansion and pricing power, supporting corporate margins and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days in non-inflationary regimes.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth exceeds 1.5% for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO signals fail to precede positive equity returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration that increases recession risk and triggers equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reduce equity exposure.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% and remains elevated for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances following UNRATE > 4.5% trigger, or Rank IC between UNRATE and forward returns exceeds -0.02 in absolute value for fewer than 3 consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A joint signal of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3.5% year-over-year) combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 3.5%) indicates restrictive monetary policy that constrains equity valuations and earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 3.5% AND FEDFUNDS > 3.5% simultaneously
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1,300 thousand units annualized signal strong residential investment demand that multiplies through construction employment and consumer wealth effects, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as forward earnings revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window, or positive HOUST surprises fail to precede positive equity returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) below 1.5% annualized signals sub-trend economic expansion that constrains earnings growth and increases recession probability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reduce equity risk exposure and reprice terminal growth rates.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 1.5% for 2 consecutive quarters
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances following sub-1.5% GDPC1 growth trigger, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and forward returns exceeds -0.02 in absolute value for fewer than 3 consecutive years.
